images.caption: Figure 1:Per-token gradient weights of different algorithms as a function of the current probabilityπ​(yt|st)\pi(y_{t}|s_{t})and behavior probabilityμ​(yt|st)\mu(y_{t}|s_{t}). For SPO,ϵ=1\epsilon=1; for DRPO,δ=1\delta=1; for PPO,εlow=0.2\varepsilon_{\rm low}=0.2andεhigh=0.28\varepsilon_{\rm high}=0.28; for DPPO,δ=0.2\delta=0.2. SPO’s weight grows without bound asμ​(yt|st)→0\mu(y_{t}|s_{t})\to 0, while the weight of DRPO remains bounded for all tokens.

Figure 2:Histogram, cumulative distribution, and absolute probability shift|π−μ||\pi-\mu|of rollout probabilitiesμ​(yt|st)\mu(y_{t}|s_{t})for tokens sampled from Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base(Yanget al.,2025). The shift|π−μ||\pi-\mu|reflects training-inference mismatch. Tokens withμ​(yt|st)≤0.01\mu(y_{t}|s_{t})\leq 0.01account for 7.8% of all sampled tokens, showing that the low-probability tail is sampled non-negligibly often.
Figure 3:Average accuracy across all main experiment settings on AIME24 and AIME25.
Figure 4:Ablation on|A^t||\hat{A}_{t}|. Removing this term degrades performance and destabilizes training.
Figure 5:Ablation on alternative divergence metrics. DRPO achieves the best performance.
